features.summary: AMD delivered record Q4 2024 revenue of $7.7 billion, up 24% year-over-year, driven by strong performance in data center and client segments. Full-year revenue reached $25.8 billion, up 14%, with data center revenue nearly doubling and client revenue growing 52%. The data center segment achieved record revenue of $3.9 billion, up 69% year-over-year, driven by both EPYC CPU and Instinct GPU growth. AMD's data center AI business exceeded $5 billion in annual revenue, establishing a multi-billion dollar franchise with strong customer adoption across hyperscalers and enterprises.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q1 2025 revenue of approximately $7.1 billion, up 30% year-over-year but down 7% sequentially due to seasonality. The company anticipates strong double-digit revenue and EPS growth for full-year 2025, with data center and client businesses driving growth while gaming and embedded show modest increases. AMD is accelerating MI350 series production to mid-year from second half, citing strong customer demand and better-than-expected silicon performance. The company sees the data center AI business growing to tens of billions of dollars annually over the coming years.
features.risks: The company faces sequential revenue decline in Q1 due to seasonality across all segments, with client and embedded businesses expected to decline more than the corporate average. Gaming segment revenue declined 59% year-over-year due to console inventory normalization, though this is expected to stabilize. The embedded segment continues to face mixed demand with slower-than-expected market recovery, particularly in industrial and communications markets. Competitive pressure in the server CPU market remains intense, with potential pricing pressures from competitors attempting to defend market share.
features.key_drivers: Data center segment growth is driven by strong EPYC CPU adoption with over 50% share at major hyperscalers and expanding enterprise deployments, plus rapid scaling of the Instinct GPU business with new customer wins and product launches. Client segment benefits from record desktop sell-out, strong Ryzen AI 300 series notebook adoption, and new commercial PC partnerships including Dell. The accelerated MI350 series launch and strong customer feedback on the MI400 series roadmap position AMD well for continued AI market expansion. ROCm software improvements and expanding ecosystem support enhance competitive positioning in AI workloads.
